---
title: "Area of Interest (AOI) Analysis for NOAA's National Database for Deep Sea Corals and Sponges"
author: "Database Version: 20260416-1"
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
output: 
  html_document:
    theme: flatly
    toc: false
    toc_float: true
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
## set global chunk options
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = FALSE, warning = FALSE, message = FALSE)

## load required modern packages
library(sf)           
library(dplyr)        
library(googledrive) 
library(knitr)
library(ggplot2)
library(rnaturalearth)
library(ggspatial) 

```

```{r authenicate}
##### authenticate #####
# Replace with the email tied to your Google Drive
drive_auth(email = "robert.mcguinn@noaa.gov")

```

```{r}
## fetch AOI Geodatabase from Google Drive
drive_id <- "10jN0aiw2l8gks7JGmcwqd2w6CXNOT8oA"

## download the zip file to a temporary location
temp_zip <- tempfile(fileext = ".zip")
drive_download(as_id(drive_id), path = temp_zip, overwrite = TRUE)

## unzip to a temporary directory
temp_dir <- tempdir()
unzip(temp_zip, exdir = temp_dir)

## find the .gdb folder
## use list.dirs() because a .gdb is a directory, not a standalone file
all_dirs <- list.dirs(temp_dir, recursive = TRUE)
gdb_path <- all_dirs[grepl("\\.gdb$", all_dirs)][1]

## safety check for GDB presence
if (is.na(gdb_path)) {
  stop("No .gdb folder was found in the downloaded zip. Please check the zip contents.")
}

## read the geodatabase into an sf object
aoi_polygon <- st_read(gdb_path, quiet = TRUE)
```

```{r sf}
## convert 'filt' to a spatial 'sf' object 
filt_sf <- st_as_sf(filt, 
                    coords = c("Longitude", "Latitude"), 
                    crs = 4326,  # WGS84 standard coordinate system
                    remove = FALSE)

## esure the coordinate reference systems (CRS) match before intersection
aoi_polygon <- st_transform(aoi_polygon, crs = st_crs(filt_sf))

## Perform the AOI intersection (keep only occurrences inside the AOI)
filt_aoi <- st_intersection(filt_sf, aoi_polygon)
```

```{r expedition_summary}
expedition_summary <- filt_aoi %>%
  st_drop_geometry() %>%
  group_by(SurveyID, Vessel, VehicleName, DatasetID, ObservationYear) %>%
  summarize(
    TotalOccurrences = n(),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  arrange(ObservationYear, SurveyID)

kable(expedition_summary, caption = "Expedition Summary within the AOI")
```

```{r taxonomic_summary}
depth_summary <- filt_aoi %>%
  st_drop_geometry() %>% 
  group_by(ScientificName, VernacularNameCategory) %>%
  summarize(
    TotalOccurrences = n(),
    MinDepth_m = min(DepthInMeters, na.rm = TRUE),
    MaxDepth_m = max(DepthInMeters, na.rm = TRUE),
    MeanDepth_m = round(mean(DepthInMeters, na.rm = TRUE), 1),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  arrange(desc(TotalOccurrences))

kable(depth_summary, caption = "Summary of Depths per Species in the AOI")
```

```{r static-map, fig.width=8, fig.height=6}
##  the US border geometry
us_border <- ne_countries(country = "United States of America", 
                          returnclass = "sf", 
                          scale = "medium")

## ensure the US border CRS matches our AOI
us_border <- st_transform(us_border, crs = st_crs(aoi_polygon))

## extract the bounding box of the AOI and set a wider buffer
aoi_bbox <- st_bbox(aoi_polygon)
zoom_buffer <- 2.0  

## build the map using ggplot2
ggplot() +
  
  geom_sf(data = us_border, fill = "gray95", color = "gray50") +
  geom_sf(data = filt_sf, color = "gray70", size = 1.0, alpha = 0.5) +
  geom_sf(data = aoi_polygon, fill = NA, color = "red", linewidth = 1.2) +
  geom_sf(data = filt_aoi, color = "dodgerblue", size = 1.5, alpha = 0.7) +
  coord_sf(
    xlim = c(aoi_bbox["xmin"] - zoom_buffer, aoi_bbox["xmax"] + zoom_buffer), 
    ylim = c(aoi_bbox["ymin"] - zoom_buffer, aoi_bbox["ymax"] + zoom_buffer),
    expand = FALSE
  ) +
  annotation_north_arrow(
    location = "tr", 
    which_north = "true", 
    pad_x = unit(0.2, "in"), 
    pad_y = unit(0.2, "in"),
    style = north_arrow_fancy_orienteering
  ) +
  annotation_scale(
    location = "bl", 
    width_hint = 0.3,
    text_cex = 0.9
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(
    title = "Biological Occurrences: Inside vs. Outside AOI",
    subtitle = "Red-AOI border; Blue- Intersecting AOI; Gray - outside AOI",
    x = "Longitude",
    y = "Latitude"
  ) +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold"),
    panel.grid.major = element_line(color = "gray90", linetype = "dashed")
  )

```

```{r export_AOI_GeoJSON}

# Export the AOI polygon to a GeoJSON file
st_write(
  obj = aoi_polygon, 
  dsn = "aoi_boundary.geojson", 
  driver = "GeoJSON", 
  delete_dsn = TRUE, # Safely overwrites the file if you run the script again
  quiet = TRUE       # Prevents console messages from printing in your HTML report
)
```

```{r import-models}
# 1. Define the local path to your zip file
zip_path <- "../indata/DSC_PredictiveModeling_US_Northeast_MidAtlantic.zip"

# Safety check: ensure the file exists before trying to unzip
if (!file.exists(zip_path)) {
  stop("The zip file was not found. Please check the 'indata' folder and path.")
}

# 2. Unzip to a temporary directory
temp_dir <- tempdir()
unzip(zip_path, exdir = temp_dir)

# 3. Find all .shp files inside the extracted folder (including subfolders)
shp_files <- list.files(temp_dir, pattern = "\\.shp$", full.names = TRUE, recursive = TRUE)

if (length(shp_files) == 0) {
  stop("No shapefiles (.shp) were found inside the zip file.")
}

# 4. Read each shapefile, transform its CRS to match the AOI, and store in a list
predictive_models <- lapply(shp_files, function(file) {
  # Read the shapefile
  temp_sf <- st_read(file, quiet = TRUE)
  
  # Transform CRS to match aoi_polygon
  temp_sf <- st_transform(temp_sf, crs = st_crs(aoi_polygon))
  
  return(temp_sf)
})

# 5. Name the items in the list based on their original filenames
names(predictive_models) <- tools::file_path_sans_ext(basename(shp_files))
```

```{r calculate-area-intersected}
# Load the units package for easy area conversions
library(units)

# Turn off strict spherical geometry
sf_use_s2(FALSE)

# Loop through each model in the list, intersect with AOI, calculate area, and summarize
area_summaries_aoi <- lapply(names(predictive_models), function(model_name) {
  
  # Extract the individual sf object
  sf_obj <- predictive_models[[model_name]]
  
  # 1. Cookie-cutter the model to the AOI boundaries
  # suppressWarnings prevents console spam about spatial attributes
  sf_intersected <- suppressWarnings(st_intersection(sf_obj, aoi_polygon))
  
  # 2. Safety check: If this specific model doesn't overlap the AOI at all, skip it
  if (nrow(sf_intersected) == 0) {
    return(NULL)
  }
  
  # 3. Calculate the area in hectares for ONLY the intersected geometries
  sf_intersected <- sf_intersected %>%
    mutate(Area_ha = as.numeric(set_units(st_area(geometry), "hectares")))
  
  # 4. Group by the suitability class (GRIDCODE) and sum the areas
  summary_table <- sf_intersected %>%
    st_drop_geometry() %>%
    group_by(GRIDCODE) %>% 
    summarize(
      Total_Area_Hectares = round(sum(Area_ha, na.rm = TRUE), 2),
      .groups = "drop"
    ) %>%
    mutate(Model_Name = model_name) # Add the model name back in
  
  return(summary_table)
})

# Combine the list of summaries into one master dataframe
# bind_rows safely ignores the NULLs from any models that were skipped
master_area_summary_aoi <- bind_rows(area_summaries_aoi)

# Reorder columns logically
master_area_summary_aoi <- master_area_summary_aoi %>%
  select(Model_Name, GRIDCODE, Total_Area_Hectares)

# Output a clean HTML table
kable(master_area_summary_aoi, caption = "Total Area (Hectares) by GRIDCODE Overlapping the AOI")

```

```{r plot-area-grid, fig.width=10, fig.height=8}
library(ggplot2)

# Create the faceted bar chart
ggplot(master_area_summary_aoi, aes(x = as.factor(GRIDCODE), y = Total_Area_Hectares, fill = as.factor(GRIDCODE))) +
  # Use geom_col for pre-summarized data (it plots exactly the Y values given)
  geom_col(color = "black", alpha = 0.8) +
  
  # Split into a grid based on Model_Name
  # scales = "free_y" allows each plot to have its own Y-axis range. 
  # If you want them all to share the exact same scale for direct visual comparison, remove that argument!
  facet_wrap(~ Model_Name, scales = "free_y") +
  
  # Clean, modern theme
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(
    title = "Total Area by Suitability Class within AOI",
    subtitle = "Area in hectares, separated by predictive model",
    x = "Suitability Class (GRIDCODE)",
    y = "Area (Hectares)"
  ) +
  
  # Format the Y-axis to use commas instead of scientific notation
  scale_y_continuous(labels = function(x) format(x, scientific = FALSE, big.mark = ",")) +
  
  # Customize text elements
  theme(
    legend.position = "none", # Hide the legend since the x-axis already shows the GRIDCODE
    strip.text = element_text(face = "bold", size = 10), # Makes the model names bold
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 0, hjust = 0.5),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank()
  )

```

```{r plot-area-grid-percent, fig.width=10, fig.height=8}
library(ggplot2)
library(units)
library(dplyr)

# 1. Calculate the total area of the AOI in hectares
# We use sum() just in case your AOI polygon is made of multiple smaller multipolygon pieces
aoi_total_area_ha <- sum(as.numeric(set_units(st_area(st_make_valid(aoi_polygon)), "hectares")))

# 2. Add the percentage column to your summary dataframe
master_area_summary_aoi <- master_area_summary_aoi %>%
  mutate(Percent_of_AOI = (Total_Area_Hectares / aoi_total_area_ha) * 100)

# 3. Create the faceted bar chart using Percent_of_AOI
ggplot(master_area_summary_aoi, aes(x = as.factor(GRIDCODE), y = Percent_of_AOI, fill = as.factor(GRIDCODE))) +
  
  geom_col(color = "black", alpha = 0.8) +
  
  # facet_wrap splits the charts by Model_Name
  # keeping scales="free_y" so small models are visible, but you can remove it to standardize axes
  facet_wrap(~ Model_Name, scales = "free_y") +
  
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(
    title = "Suitability Class Coverage within the AOI",
    subtitle = paste0("Percentage of total AOI area (Total AOI = ", round(aoi_total_area_ha, 0), " ha)"),
    x = "Suitability Class (GRIDCODE)",
    y = "Percent of AOI (%)"
  ) +
  
  # Format the Y-axis to dynamically show the % sign and round to 1 decimal
  scale_y_continuous(labels = function(x) paste0(round(x, 1), "%")) +
  
  theme(
    legend.position = "none", 
    strip.text = element_text(face = "bold", size = 10), 
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 0, hjust = 0.5),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank()
  )
```
